name: ChatGPT

paidPlans.name: Plus

name: Claude

paidPlans.details: More usage, Claude Opus, priority access

pros: Best for analysis and formal documents
Claude Code is top coding tool

paidPlans.details: Per user, team features, admin controls

name: Aider
description: AI pair programming in your terminal. Works with any LLM. Git-aware edits.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Open source, bring your own API key

name: Continue
description: Open-source AI code assistant. VS Code and JetBrains. Bring your own model.

name: Cody (Sourcegraph)
description: AI coding assistant with full codebase context. Enterprise-grade. Code search.
cons: Requires Sourcegraph setup for full power

name: Napkin AI
description: Converts text into visual diagrams and presentations. Bridge between text AI and design tools.

name: Gamma
description: AI presentation and document maker. Beautiful slides from text prompts.

name: Mem
description: AI-powered knowledge base. Auto-organizes notes. Finds connections between ideas.

name: Taskade
description: AI-powered productivity. Task management, notes, mind maps, AI agents.

name: Beautiful.ai
description: AI presentation maker. Smart templates, auto-formatting. No design skills needed.

name: Activepieces
description: MIT-licensed automation platform. n8n alternative with lower vendor risk.

name: Pipedream
description: Developer-first automation. Code + low-code. 2000+ integrations. Generous free tier.
cons: Requires coding knowledge for best use

name: LiteLLM
description: Multi-AI gateway. One API for 100+ models. Zero markup. CAUTION: Supply chain attack March 2026.

name: RouteLLM
description: Smart model routing for cost optimization. 85% cost reduction by routing to appropriate model.

name: NVIDIA Isaac Sim
description: Open-source robotics simulation on Omniverse. GPU-accelerated physics, synthetic data generation, RL training, ROS 2 integration, digital twins. The dominant sim-to-real platform in 2026.

name: NVIDIA Isaac Lab
description: GPU-accelerated robot learning framework built on Isaac Sim. 16+ robot models, 30+ environments. Supports RL, imitation learning, motion planning with RSL RL, SKRL, Stable Baselines.

name: MuJoCo
description: High-performance physics engine for robotics and RL research. Gold standard for contact-rich simulation. MuJoCo Warp (GPU) and MuJoCo Playground actively updated. Originally by Emo Todorov, now open-sourced by DeepMind.

freeTierLimits.notes: Free and open source (Apache 2.0). No limits.

name: Gazebo (Harmonic)
description: ROS 2-native robot simulator for multi-robot setups, sensor simulation, and navigation stacks. The standard simulator in the ROS ecosystem. Uses SDF/URDF assets.

freeTierLimits.notes: Free and open source. No limits.

name: NVIDIA GR00T N1
description: World's first open humanoid robot foundation model. Dual-system architecture (fast reflexes + deliberate reasoning). Cross-embodiment manipulation. Adopted by 1X, Agility, Boston Dynamics, NEURA.

name: LeRobot
description: Open-source robot learning platform. 22.7K GitHub stars. Models, datasets (grew from 1,145 to 26,991 on HF Hub), and tools in PyTorch. v0.5 adds Unitree G1 support, Pi0-FAST VLA policies, Isaac Lab integration. Accepted at ICLR 2026.

name: Physical Intelligence pi0
description: Flow-matching VLA model for general robot control. Built on PaliGemma (3B params). Demonstrated folding laundry, packing, cooking. Outperforms OpenVLA and Octo on benchmarks. Now open-sourced. $400M+ funded.

name: Open X-Embodiment Dataset
description: Largest open robotics dataset: 1M+ real robot trajectories spanning 22 robot embodiments from 34 labs. Foundation for training cross-embodiment models like RT-X and OpenVLA.

name: PX4 Autopilot
description: Open-source production-grade flight control. AI/vision integration, LLM-driven autonomy via ROS 2, RL policy embedding. Powers Amazon MK30 and Zipline Sparrow. v1.16 adds ROS 2/fastDDS bridging.

name: ArduPilot
description: Versatile open-source autopilot for multi-copters, fixed wing, boats, rovers. 12.1K GitHub stars, 1.5K+ contributors. Dominates large-payload ag-spray VTOLs. Plane 4.6 added dual-IMU EKF3.

name: Unitree Robotics SDK
description: ROS 2 tools for G1, H1, Go2, R1 robots. Open-sourced VLA and world-model-action models. MuJoCo simulation. LeRobot integration. G1 from $13.5K, R1 from $4.9K — most accessible humanoid platform.

name: Boston Dynamics Spot SDK + Orbit
description: Python SDK for Spot control, autonomy, payloads. Orbit fleet management with REST API, AI-powered anomaly detection. Electric Atlas humanoid in pilot at Hyundai.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: SDK free; requires Spot hardware (~$108K CAD)

name: NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin
description: Flagship edge AI computer: 275 TOPS, 12-core ARM, 2048-core Ampere GPU, up to 64GB. 15-60W. Full CUDA ecosystem. Runs GR00T, Nemotron, Qwen. Industrial-grade option with ECC (-40C to 85C). Dominant edge AI for robotics.

name: Edge Impulse
description: End-to-end TinyML development platform. Data collection, model training, optimization (EON Compiler reduces RAM 25-55%), deployment to MCUs. Supports Arduino, ESP32, STM32, Jetson. Leading TinyML platform.

freeTierLimits.notes: Free: 5 projects, 100K inferences/month. Enterprise for more.

name: NVIDIA Isaac ROS
description: CUDA-accelerated ROS 2 packages: Visual SLAM, nvBlox (3D mapping), cuMotion (motion planning), FoundationPose (6D pose estimation). GPU-accelerated drop-ins for the ROS ecosystem.

name: Nav2 (Navigation2)
description: The ROS 2 navigation stack. Path planning (A*, Dijkstra), local obstacle avoidance (DWA, TEB), behavior trees, waypoint following. Industry standard for autonomous mobile robot navigation.

name: Foxglove
description: Modern robotics data visualization and observability. 20+ panels for debugging live/recorded ROS 2 data. Supports MCAP, .bag, .db3. Browser and desktop. Better than RViz for large datasets.

name: RealSense Depth Cameras
description: Depth cameras with mm-level accuracy. Powers 60% of global AMR market and 80% of humanoid robotics. 276M data points/sec. Spun out from Intel July 2025 with $50M. NVIDIA Jetson Thor partnership.

name: Luxonis OAK-D / DepthAI
description: AI depth cameras with on-device neural inference (4 TOPS). Spatial AI fusing object detection with depth perception on-camera. OAK 4 is first fully self-contained AI vision platform. ROS driver available.

name: Covariant Brain (RFM-1)
description: Universal AI for robot picking. Trained on the largest multimodal warehouse robotics dataset. Robots pick virtually any SKU on Day One. $222M funded. Warehouse/logistics focus.

name: Wandelbots NOVA
description: Hardware-agnostic robot OS with no-code teaching. Programs robots 20x faster, 10x more cost-effective. Isaac Sim integration. Supports UR, Yaskawa, more. Customers: VW, Bayer, Fraunhofer. $123M funded.

name: Intrinsic Flowstate (Google)
description: Web-based platform for building robotic applications without thousands of lines of code. Uses Gemini models and Google Cloud. AI for Industry developer challenge. Merged into Google 2025.

name: Meta Habitat 3.0
description: High-quality simulator for robots and humanoid avatars in human-robot collaboration research. Social navigation benchmarks, PARTNR benchmark for planning and reasoning evaluation.

name: Drake
description: Toolbox for multibody dynamics, hydroelastic contact, model-based control, analysis-grade robotics simulation. Strong in manipulation planning. Used by TRI, MIT, Stanford.

name: Webots
description: Open-source robot simulator since 1998. Good ROS 2 support, ideal for education, rapid prototyping, swarm robotics. Runs on modest hardware.

name: Anthropic Claude API
description: Programmatic access to Claude models (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku) via REST API. Supports text, vision, tool use, and streaming. Pay-per-token pricing.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Free tier with limited credits for new accounts

paidPlans.name: Pay-as-you-go
paidPlans.details: Opus: ~$21.60/$108 per MTok in/out; Sonnet: ~$4.32/$21.60; Haiku: ~$0.36/$1.80 (CAD)

freeTierLimits.notes: No free tier. Pay-per-token. See anthropic.com/pricing

pros: Full programmatic control over Claude models
Tool use and structured output support
cons: Costs scale with usage — can get expensive at volume

name: OpenAI API
description: Comprehensive AI API offering GPT-4o/GPT-5, DALL-E 3, Whisper, TTS, embeddings, and fine-tuning. The most widely integrated AI API with extensive third-party ecosystem.

freeTierLimits.notes: No free tier. Requires prepaid credits. Minimum $5 USD.

pros: Widest model selection (text, image, audio, embeddings)
cons: Rate limits can be restrictive on lower tiers
Data usage policies require careful review

name: Google Gemini API
description: Multimodal AI API via Google AI Studio or Vertex AI. Access Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash models with text, image, video, and audio understanding. Generous free tier.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Generous free tier via Google AI Studio (rate-limited)

paidPlans.name: Pay-as-you-go
paidPlans.details: Gemini 2.5 Pro: ~$1.80/$14.40 per MTok in/out (CAD). Free tier available.

freeTierLimits.grounding: 500 RPD with Google Search (shared with Flash-Lite)
freeTierLimits.notes: Free tier, no billing required

pros: Very generous free tier for prototyping
Deep integration with Google Cloud ecosystem
cons: Vertex AI setup can be complex

name: Perplexity API
description: Search-augmented AI API that combines LLM reasoning with real-time web search. Returns cited, up-to-date answers. Ideal for research and fact-checking pipelines.

paidPlans.name: Pay-as-you-go
paidPlans.details: Sonar Pro: ~$4.32/$21.60 per MTok in/out + $7.20/1K searches (CAD)

freeTierLimits.notes: Pay-per-token. No free credits for new accounts.

pros: Simple API — no need to build RAG pipelines
cons: No free tier for API access
Search costs add up at scale

name: OpenAI DALL-E API
description: Image generation API endpoint supporting DALL-E 3 and GPT Image 1.5. Create, edit, and vary images programmatically. Integrated into many third-party apps.
pros: Easy API integration for image generation
cons: No free tier — pay per image
Content policy restrictions can be aggressive

name: Suno API
description: Music generation API for creating songs from text prompts. Generates vocals, instrumentals, and full productions. Used for automated music creation pipelines.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Limited free generations via web interface

pros: Full song generation from text prompts
cons: API access may require higher-tier plan
Limited fine control over musical structure

name: Runway API
description: Video generation API providing programmatic access to Gen-3 Alpha and other Runway models. Create videos from text or images via REST API.
pros: Active development with frequent model updates
cons: Credits consumed quickly for video generation
Generation times can be slow for longer clips

name: GitHub Actions
description: CI/CD and workflow automation platform built into GitHub. Run scheduled tasks, deploy code, validate data, and automate any software workflow. Free for public repos.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: 2,000 minutes/month free for public repos, 500 min for private

freeTierLimits.notes: 2000 minutes/month free for public repos. 500 for private.

pros: Native GitHub integration — triggers on push, PR, schedule
cons: Minutes quota burns fast on private repos

name: GitHub Pages
description: Free static site hosting directly from GitHub repositories. Supports custom domains, HTTPS, and Jekyll. Perfect for dashboards, docs, and project sites.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Free hosting for public repos, 100GB bandwidth/month

freeTierLimits.notes: Free for public repos. 100GB bandwidth, 1GB storage per site.

cons: Static sites only — no server-side code
Build times can be slow for large sites

name: Claude Scheduled Tasks
description: Recurring Claude prompts that run automatically on Anthropic servers. Execute research, data updates, and analysis on a schedule without manual intervention.

paidPlans.name: Included with Pro
paidPlans.details: Available as part of Claude Pro subscription

pros: Full Claude capabilities including web search

name: Puppeteer
description: Headless Chrome/Chromium browser automation library for Node.js. Scrape web pages, generate PDFs, take screenshots, and automate browser interactions programmatically.
pros: Excellent for web scraping and testing
cons: High memory usage for many concurrent pages
Stealth mode needed to avoid bot detection

name: Playwright
description: Cross-browser automation framework supporting Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit. Modern alternative to Puppeteer with auto-waiting, tracing, and parallel execution.
cons: Slightly steeper learning curve than Puppeteer

name: FastAPI
description: Modern Python web framework for building APIs. Automatic OpenAPI docs, async support, type validation via Pydantic. One of the fastest Python frameworks available.
cons: Async patterns can be confusing for beginners

name: Cloudflare Tunnel
description: Expose local services to the internet securely without port forwarding. Free tier available. Useful for webhooks, n8n, and local AI model serving.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Free for personal use with Cloudflare account

paidPlans.name: Zero Trust (Teams)
paidPlans.details: Per user, advanced access controls and logging

pros: No port forwarding or firewall changes needed
cons: Requires Cloudflare account and DNS setup
Can add latency for some use cases

name: Blender
description: Industry-standard open-source 3D creation suite. Modeling, sculpting, animation, rendering, compositing, and video editing. Massive addon ecosystem including AI plugins.

freeTierLimits.notes: Fully free and open source. No limits.

pros: Completely free — no subscription or license fees
cons: Some CAD/engineering features are less polished than dedicated tools

name: FreeCAD
description: Open-source parametric 3D CAD modeler for engineering and product design. Supports STEP, IGES, STL, and many other formats. Python scriptable.

freeTierLimits.notes: Fully free and open source. No limits.

pros: Free parametric CAD — rare in engineering software
cons: Less polished UI than commercial alternatives
Can be unstable with complex models
Smaller community than Blender or Fusion 360

name: Autodesk Fusion 360
description: Professional cloud-based CAD/CAM/CAE platform. Parametric modeling, simulation, generative design, and CNC toolpath generation. Industry standard for product design.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Free for personal/hobby use with limited features

paidPlans.name: Commercial
paidPlans.details: Full features including simulation and generative design

pros: Free hobby tier is very capable
cons: Can be slow with very large assemblies

name: OrcaSlicer
description: Open-source 3D print slicer forked from Bambu Studio. Supports most FDM printers. CLI mode enables automated slicing pipelines. Advanced multi-material support.
pros: Free with excellent Bambu Lab printer support

name: LTspice
description: Industry-standard SPICE circuit simulator. Schematic capture, waveform viewer, and simulation of analog/mixed-signal circuits. Completely free with no limits.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Completely free with no feature restrictions

pros: Huge component library from Analog Devices
cons: Schematic editor is less intuitive than modern tools

name: JLCPCB
description: Low-cost PCB manufacturing service with API ordering capabilities. Supports PCB fabrication, SMT assembly, and 3D printing. Popular for prototyping and small batch production.

paidPlans.name: Per-order
paidPlans.details: Starting ~$2.88 CAD for 5 PCBs (2-layer). Assembly from ~$11.52 CAD setup.

cons: Shipping from China adds time and cost
Quality varies — inspect critical boards carefully

name: DaVinci Resolve
description: Professional video editing, color grading, VFX, and audio post-production suite. Free version includes nearly all features. Industry standard for color grading.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Free version with most professional features included

paidPlans.name: Studio
paidPlans.details: One-time purchase. Neural Engine AI tools, HDR, stereoscopic 3D, multi-GPU.

pros: All-in-one: edit, color, VFX, audio, delivery
cons: Some AI features require paid Studio version

name: Coqui TTS
description: Open-source text-to-speech library supporting multiple languages and voice cloning. Runs locally for privacy. Includes XTTS v2 for high-quality multilingual synthesis.
pros: Voice cloning from short audio samples
cons: Requires decent GPU for real-time synthesis

name: Twilio
description: Cloud communication APIs for voice calls, SMS, video, and messaging. Programmable voice enables AI-powered phone systems. Widely used for automated communication.

paidPlans.name: Pay-as-you-go
paidPlans.details: Voice: ~$0.02 CAD/min, SMS: ~$0.01 CAD/msg, Phone numbers: ~$1.50 CAD/mo

pros: Supports voice, SMS, video, and WhatsApp
cons: Costs add up quickly at scale
Phone number regulations vary by country

name: Microsoft Power Automate
description: Low-code workflow automation platform. Connect Microsoft 365, third-party apps, and AI services. Desktop flows for RPA, cloud flows for API automation.

paidPlans.name: Premium
paidPlans.details: Per user, all connectors, AI Builder credits

pros: Desktop RPA for legacy application automation
cons: Can be slow for complex workflows

name: Toggl Track
description: Simple time tracking tool for individuals and teams. One-click timer, detailed reports, and project-based tracking. Integrates with 100+ tools.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Free for up to 5 users with basic tracking

paidPlans.name: Starter
paidPlans.details: Per user, billable rates, project time estimates

paidPlans.name: Premium
paidPlans.details: Per user, time audits, scheduled reports

pros: Free tier is generous for individuals

name: RescueTime
description: Automatic time tracking and productivity monitoring. Runs in the background categorizing app and website usage. Focus sessions and distraction blocking.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Free Lite version with basic tracking

paidPlans.name: Premium
paidPlans.details: Detailed reports, focus sessions, distraction blocking, goal setting

pros: Completely automatic — no manual tracking needed
cons: Privacy concern — monitors all computer activity
Can feel surveillance-like for team use

name: PubMed API (E-utilities)
description: Free API for searching and retrieving biomedical literature from PubMed's 36M+ citations. E-utilities provide programmatic access to search, fetch, and link biomedical data.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Completely free. 3 requests/sec without API key, 10/sec with key.

pros: Completely free access to 36M+ biomedical citations
cons: Rate-limited — need API key for higher throughput
Search syntax has a learning curve

name: ClinicalTrials.gov API
description: Free API for searching the world's largest clinical trial registry (400K+ studies). Query by condition, intervention, location, and status. Essential for medical research.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Completely free with no authentication required

pros: Completely free with no API key needed
JSON responses — modern and easy to parse
cons: Data quality varies — submitted by sponsors
Complex query syntax for advanced searches
Some fields may be incomplete or outdated

name: Google Scholar Alerts
description: Email notifications for new academic papers matching search queries or citing specific papers. Free monitoring of research topics. No API — email-based alerts.
pros: Covers vast majority of scholarly publications
Citation alerts for tracking paper impact
cons: Can miss some publications — coverage gaps exist

name: YouTube
description: World's largest video platform with emerging AI features. Auto-captions, Dream Screen AI backgrounds, video summarization. Essential distribution channel for AI-generated content.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Free to upload and watch, ad-supported

paidPlans.name: Premium
paidPlans.details: Ad-free, background play, YouTube Music, downloads

pros: Largest video audience in the world

name: KiCad
description: Open-source electronics design suite: schematic capture, PCB layout, 3D viewer, Gerber export. Industry standard for open-source PCB design.

freeTierLimits.notes: Desktop app, no limits

cons: Steeper learning curve than commercial tools

name: OpenCV
description: Open-source computer vision library. Image processing, object detection, face recognition, optical flow. Foundation of most CV pipelines in robotics.

name: Groq
description: Ultra-fast LLM inference on custom LPU hardware. Runs Llama, Mixtral at 10-100x speed of GPU inference. Free tier: 30 req/min, 14,400 req/day.

name: Tavily
description: AI search API for agents. Real-time web search, content extraction, crawling. Returns structured results with relevance scores. 1000 free credits/month.

freeTierLimits.notes: Search=1 credit, Advanced=5

cons: 1000 credits goes fast with daily use

name: Mistral AI
description: European AI. Mistral Large/Small/Codestral models. OpenAI-compatible API. Strong multilingual support. Open-weight models available.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Free Experiment plan (data used for research)

freeTierLimits.notes: Requires phone verification

name: ROS 2 (Robot Operating System)
description: Standard middleware for robotics. Handles communication between sensors, actuators, planners, AI modules. ROS 2 Humble/Iron/Jazzy for production robots.

name: Hunyuan Video
description: Largest open-source video model (13B params). v1.5 runs on a single RTX 4090. Free online studio. API via fal.ai. High fidelity, no audio generation.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Open-source, free online studio, API ~$0.075/sec via fal.ai

freeTierLimits.notes: Open-source. Self-host free. Cloud API is pay-per-second.

name: LTX Studio / LTX-2
description: Open-source video model with native 4K + synchronized audio generation. Licensed training data (Getty/Shutterstock) = clean IP chain. Runs on 16GB consumer GPUs.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: 800 one-time credits on LTX Studio. Model free for companies under $10M ARR.

freeTierLimits.notes: 800 one-time credits. Does not renew on free plan.

cons: Full studio features need paid plan

name: Vadoo AI
description: All-in-one short-form content factory: AI scriptwriting, text-to-video, captions, voiceovers, B-roll, SEO tools, auto-posting to TikTok/Reels/Shorts.

freeTierLimits.notes: 20 videos/month renewable. 200MB storage. 1m30s max per video.

cons: Lower quality than Kling/Veo for cinematic content

name: Higgsfield AI
description: Multi-model video aggregator — 15+ models (Veo, Kling, Seedance) under one subscription with 70+ cinematic camera presets.

freeTierLimits.notes: Limited free tier. Details unclear.

name: Seedance
description: ByteDance's video AI. 2K resolution, 24fps, up to 15 seconds. Integrating into CapCut (rolling out, not US yet). Consumer access via Dreamina.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: ~225 daily free tokens via Dreamina (~1-2 videos/day, watermarked)

freeTierLimits.notes: 225 daily tokens via Dreamina. Watermarked. Renews daily.

cons: Not available in US yet (CapCut integration)

name: Pollo AI
description: Multi-model video aggregator (Pollo 2.5, Kling, Veo, Seedance). Draft-then-finalize workflow for cost optimization.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: 10 credits, 1 video, 10 images. No quality throttling on free tier.

freeTierLimits.notes: 10 credits = 1 video. Same quality as paid. Does not auto-renew.

name: A2E
description: Avatar and face pipeline — AI avatars, face swap, head swap, voice cloning (50+ languages), lip sync, talking photos.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: ~30 daily credits (watermarked 720p, lower priority). 100 bonus credits for new users.

freeTierLimits.notes: 30 daily credits renewable. Watermarked 720p. 100 bonus credits on signup.

name: Hailuo AI
description: Budget champion for AI video. MiniMax's consumer brand. Hailuo 2.3 set new cost-effectiveness record. 6-second 1080p clips with free tier.

pricingCAD.freeTierDetails: Free tier: 6-second 1080p watermarked videos, ~20-30 short clips, daily free credits during launch.

freeTierLimits.notes: Daily free credits. Watermarked. 6-second 1080p.

pros: Best price/quality ratio in budget segment
